query: US Iran ceasefire negotiations June 2026 14-point memorandum
answer:
The research agent found comprehensive data from local research files. Here is the full synthesis:

---

# US-Iran Ceasefire Negotiations & the 14-Point Memorandum — June 2026

## Background: The 2026 Iran War

The ceasefire negotiations stem from the **2026 Iran War**, which began on **February 28, 2026** when the US and Israel launched **"Operation Midnight Hammer"** — coordinated large-scale strikes on Iran that assassinated **Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei** (confirmed dead March 1), destroyed nuclear facilities at **Fordow, Natanz, and Isfahan**, struck over **11,000 targets**, killed **3,000+ Iranians**, and resulted in 15 US soldiers killed and 543 wounded. Iran retaliated by **closing the Strait of Hormuz** — described by S&P Global as the biggest disruption in modern energy history, affecting ~25% of world seaborne oil and ~20% of global LNG.

**Mojtaba Khamenei** (son of the assassinated Supreme Leader) was selected as the new Supreme Leader on March 8–9, 2026.

---

## The 14-Point Memorandum

On **May 2, 2026**, Iran submitted a **14-point ceasefire proposal** to the United States through mediators. **Trump rejected this proposal.** Per Iran's Mehr News Agency, the 14-point draft memorandum of understanding included:

- A **30-day resolution timeline**
- **Full US military withdrawal** from the region
- **End to the US naval blockade** of Iranian ports
- **All sanctions lifted**
- **War reparations** estimated at $300 billion to $1 trillion
- **Release of $24 billion in frozen Iranian assets**, with half ($12 billion) released before substantive nuclear talks begin

VP Vance explicitly denied the $24 billion figure: *"That just doesn't appear anywhere in any of the texts that we've talked about with the Iranians."* The IRGC and Iranian state media have continued referencing the 14-point framework even as the actual deal text evolved.

---

## The "Islamabad Memorandum" — The Actual Deal

The deal that emerged is formally called the **"Islamabad Memorandum"**, described by VP Vance as **"a very general document" roughly "a page and a half" long**. Key terms include:

1. **End to military strikes** between the US and Iran
2. **Reopening of the Strait of Hormuz** to commercial shipping without tolls
3. **End to the US naval blockade** of Iranian ports
4. **60-day ceasefire extension** for further negotiations
5. **$300 billion investment fund** for Iran (per Reuters; Vance said funded by Gulf states, not US; Trump denied the figure)
6. **Full restoration of pre-war shipping** within ~30 days
7. Iran agreeing **not to produce/acquire nuclear weapons** and to dilute its HEU stockpile

**Notably absent**: specifics on Iran's nuclear program, ballistic missile program, proxy network, or Lebanon.

---

## Negotiation Timeline

| Date | Event |
|------|-------|
| Feb 6–26, 2026 | Three rounds of pre-war talks (Muscat, Geneva) |
| **Feb 28** | **War begins — Operation Midnight Hammer** |
| Apr 7–8 | Two-week ceasefire brokered by Pakistan |
| **Apr 11–12** | **Islamabad Talks** — first direct US-Iran talks since 1979 (21 hours; collapsed) |
| Apr 13 | US imposes naval blockade |
| **May 2** | **Iran submits 14-point ceasefire proposal (rejected)** |
| May 4–7 | Major escalation: Iran attacks UAE; US sinks IRGC boats, strikes Bandar Abbas |
| May 17 | Trump sets 5 preconditions for deal |
| May 22 | Qatar joins Pakistan as co-mediator |
| Jun 10–11 | IRGC declares "complete closure" of Strait; strike exchanges |
| **Jun 14** | **Deal digitally signed; both sides confirm** |
| **Jun 19** | **Formal signing ceremony scheduled — Burgenstock Resort, Switzerland** |

---

## Parties Involved

- **US**: VP JD Vance (lead negotiator), Steve Witkoff, Jared Kushner, Admiral Brad Cooper, Sec. Rubio, Sec. Hegseth
- **Iran**: FM Abbas Araghchi, Deputy FM Gharibabadi, Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf, Deputy FM Takht-Ravanchi
- **Mediators**: Pakistan (primary — PM Sharif, FM Asim Munir), Qatar (co-mediator from May 22), Saudi Arabia, Turkey

---

## Current Status (as of June 17, 2026)

- MOU **digitally signed June 14**; formal ceremony at **Burgenstock Resort, Switzerland on June 19**
- US naval blockade **remains in effect** until formal signing
- 60-day window for substantive nuclear/sanctions negotiations to begin
- **No UN Security Council resolution** endorsing the deal
- **Critical risk**: IRGC has **NOT publicly endorsed the deal** despite operationally controlling the Strait closure
- **Israel not party** to the agreement; Netanyahu struck Beirut on June 14 despite Trump warnings

## Prediction Markets (June 15, 2026)

- **Metaculus**: 75% probability of formal signing before June 26
- **Metaculus**: Only 40% probability of Strait actually reopening before June 27
- **Polymarket**: ~81.5% probability of signed agreement by July 31

## Economic Impact

- Brent crude: pre-war ~$72.50/bbl → peak $126.10/bbl → post-deal ~$83.63/bbl
- US gasoline: $2.98/gal → peak $4.45/gal (+49%)
- Strait of Hormuz closed ~108 days as of reporting
- Estimated US war cost: $25–29 billion
- Goldman Sachs 12-month recession probability: 30%

key_facts: On May 2, 2026, Iran submitted a 14-point ceasefire proposal to the US through mediators; Trump rejected it.

The 14-point memorandum demanded: 30-day resolution timeline, full US military withdrawal, end to naval blockade, all sanctions lifted, $300B-$1T war reparations, and release of $24B in frozen assets.
VP Vance explicitly denied the $24 billion frozen assets figure appeared in any negotiation texts.
The actual deal that emerged is the 'Islamabad Memorandum' — described by Vance as roughly a page and a half long.
The Islamabad Memorandum was digitally signed on June 14, 2026 by both sides.
A formal signing ceremony is scheduled for June 19, 2026 at the Burgenstock Resort in Switzerland.

Key deal terms: end to strikes, reopening Strait of Hormuz without tolls, end to naval blockade, 60-day ceasefire extension, $300B investment fund (disputed), Iran agrees not to produce/acquire nuclear weapons.
The deal does NOT cover Iran's nuclear program specifics, ballistic missiles, proxy networks, or Lebanon.
The 2026 Iran War began February 28 with Operation Midnight Hammer; Supreme Leader Khamenei was assassinated; Mojtaba Khamenei succeeded him March 8-9.
Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz — affecting ~25% of world seaborne oil and ~20% of global LNG.
Pakistan served as primary mediator; Qatar joined as co-mediator May 22, 2026.
The Islamabad Talks (April 11-12) were the first direct US-Iran talks since 1979 — 21 hours, collapsed without deal.
IRGC has NOT publicly endorsed the deal despite operationally controlling the Strait of Hormuz closure — assessed as critical risk.
Israel is not party to the agreement and disputes that the ceasefire covers its Lebanon operations.
52 US senators and 177 representatives signed a letter opposing any deal allowing continued Iranian enrichment.
Metaculus: 75% probability of formal signing before June 26; only 40% probability of Strait actually reopening before June 27.
Polymarket: ~81.5% probability of signed agreement by July 31.
